package.name: ikt_inverse_kinematics
package.description: Robot-agnostic inverse-kinematics service for the cartesian_controllers toolkit. Given a URDF (read live from /robot_description) it solves, for one or more chosen links (including intermediate links and virtual tool frames, and multiple tips for a dual-arm robot), the joint angles that place those links at requested target poses -- subject to joint limits, singularity robustness, a soft rest-posture bias, and per-DOF task stiffness. It also reports/controls the 7-DOF arm-angle (null-space) redundancy, a reachability verdict with closest-reachable pose, accepts TF-framed targets, and supports a dual-arm relative-pose constraint. The solver is ADVISORY ONLY: it publishes IK results (joint-angle solutions + diagnostics); it never commands the robot. A headless solver node, an optional web dashboard, an optional RViz interactive-marker bridge, and a CLI are provided.
